name: Texan Fried Pies
recipeInstructions:
1. Place apricots with enough water to cover in a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes. Drain and mash.
2. Stir in sugar, lemon juice, cinnamon, and nutmeg. Set aside.
3. Make the pastry while the apricots are cooking. In a large mixing bowl, combine flour and salt. Cut in shortening with a pastry blender until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
4. Combine egg and water; sprinkle over the flour mixture. Add vinegar and lightly stir with a fork until the mixture forms a ball. Wrap the pastry in wax paper and chill for at least 1 hour, or until ready to use.
5. Roll dough to 1/8 inch thickness on a lightly floured surface. Cut into circles (4-6 inches).
6. Place 1 or 2 tablespoons of apricot mixture in the center of each pastry circle. Moisten the edges of the pastry and fold them over, making sure they are even.
7. Press the edges of the pastry together using a fork dipped in flour.
8. Heat 3/4 inch of oil to 375°F (190°C) in a large skillet. Cook the pies until golden brown on both sides, turning once. Drain well on paper towels.
9. Sprinkle with powdered sugar, granulated sugar, or a sugar/cinnamon mixture.
